properties.titulo_evento: Italy busts Russian spy ring collecting data on Ukrainian air defense vulnerabilities
properties.descricao: Russia has told one of its spies in Europe to find out more about air defense capabilities Western nations are sending to Ukraine.

properties.descricao: Details were scant about a project that may amount to another European weapons-coordination umbrella rather than new technology development.

properties.descricao: Germany will purchase Tomahawk cruise missiles from the United States and station them on German soil.

properties.descricao: The office of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez said it was treating Trump’s statements as business as usual, adding bilateral relations benefited both nations.

properties.descricao: Delcy Rodriguez is courting Israel only to secure Washington’s support and undercut her main rival.

properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump gave his blessing earlier this week to domestic Ukrainian production of the missiles.

properties.descricao: Iranian state media reported that military headquarters were hit by strikes in Bushehr province and the city of Konarak.

properties.descricao: Israeli strike killed Mohammed al-Wahidi and three others on the eve of the Egypt vs Argentina match on Tuesday.

properties.titulo_evento: Britain’s likely PM says will work to ‘stop the suffering’ in Gaza
properties.descricao: Andy Burnham took to social media to apologise for the Labour Party’s initial stance on Israeli attacks in Gaza.

properties.titulo_evento: China expands anti-sanctions toolkit, raising risks for foreign firms
properties.descricao: Beijing is rolling out measures to expand its ability to hit back at US and EU sanctions and export controls.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: Uneasy calm in the Middle East after deadly attacks
properties.descricao: American official says technical talks with Tehran continue and the United States wants to pursue diplomacy.

properties.descricao: Aid worker Mohammed al-Wahidi had become a prominent humanitarian figure during the Israel-Hamas war.

properties.titulo_evento: China lands reusable rocket for first time, state media says
properties.descricao: It follows similar landings of reusable rockets by US-owned companies SpaceX and Blue Origin.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
properties.descricao: The attacks appear to be the latest phase of Ukraine's bid to choke off supplies and routes into and out of occupied Crimea.

properties.titulo_evento: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato
properties.descricao: The weapon has not been brought back to the UK and has instead been left with British officials in Turkey.

properties.titulo_evento: What's next for Iran after the funeral of their late supreme leader?
properties.descricao: The BBC's chief international correspondent looks at how Iran's new leadership will respond to several issues affecting the country.

properties.titulo_evento: Bayeux Tapestry arrives safely in UK after loan from France
properties.descricao: The medieval Bayeux Tapestry arrived in London after almost 1,000 years early on Friday. The tapestry is on loan from France for a year and will be exhibited at the British Museum. Its journey has been surrounded by secrecy due to security concerns....

properties.titulo_evento: N. Korea says will bolster nuclear force, expand S. Korea spy activities in ‘radical way’
properties.descricao: North Korea plans to boost its nuclear force “both in quality and quantity”, state media reported on Friday, citing a decision from a military commission meeting. The country also announced plans to bolster its spying activities in South Korea “in a...

properties.titulo_evento: US-Iran conflict escalates as fresh missile strikes threaten Middle East ceasefire
properties.descricao: The United States launched fresh air strikes on Iran early Thursday, prompting Tehran to fire missiles at its neighbours Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait and Jordan hosting US military forces, officials said. The exchanges threatened a fragile interim ceasefi...

properties.titulo_evento: Spain wildfire kills several, with some victims found in vehicles

properties.titulo_evento: Live: Iran buries Ali Khamenei after new fighting with US erupts

properties.titulo_evento: Technical details need to be agreed before Ukraine can produce Patriot missiles, Zelensky says
properties.descricao: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday said that technical details still needed to be finalised with the US before Kyiv could start manufacturing its own Patriot air-defence missiles. Ukraine's dwindling interceptor stockpiles have been ...

properties.titulo_evento: Gazans mourn aid worker killed by Israel who organised public World Cup screenings
properties.descricao: Crowds of Gazans took to the streets on Wednesday for the funeral of Palestinian aid worker Mohammad al-Waheidi, who was killed by an Israeli air strike Tuesday. Waheidi had been involved in setting up public screenings of World Cup matches in the d...

properties.titulo_evento: Ukrainian drone strikes ignite fires at Russian oil sites
properties.descricao: An overnight assault reached key oil facilities in southern Russia, adding pressure to a fuel network already strained by repeated Ukrainian strikes.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: Former supreme leader buried amid new escalation
properties.descricao: Iran buried the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei following six days of funeral proceedings and amid a renewed escalation. Meanwhile, some tankers continue transiting the Strait of Hormuz. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in British Museum for historic exhibition
properties.descricao: After nearly 1,000 years, the medieval masterpiece is back on British soil. The Bayeux Tapestry was transferred from France to London in a highly secretive operation and will be on display in September.

properties.titulo_evento: Spain battles deadly wildfire amid heat wave
properties.descricao: Spain has mobilized military emergency units to work with regional firefighters to control the blaze. The Spanish royal family and Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez expressed their deep sorrow over the loss of life.

properties.titulo_evento: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
properties.descricao: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

properties.titulo_evento: China: Dozens killed in shoe factory fire
properties.descricao: Hundreds of rescue workers were dispatched to the site in Jinjiang, China, where many had become trapped in a burning factory. President Xi Jinping said "heavy human losses" were expected.

properties.titulo_evento: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
properties.descricao: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

properties.titulo_evento: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
properties.descricao: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelenskyy: China urged Putin not to use nuclear weapons in Ukraine
properties.descricao: The Ukrainian president said he learned of Beijing's intervention from European leaders at the NATO summit in Ankara.

properties.descricao: Investors have taken heart from the fragile cease-fire between the United States and Iran before. Will they do so again?

properties.descricao: Oil prices are likely to remain volatile as long as the United States and Iran are unable to resolve the status of the Strait of Hormuz.
